summary: This brief post serves as a double announcement. Spolsky teases his foreword to Rick Chapman's book 'In Search of Stupidity,' which explores the tension between technical and business people in tech companies. He also announces the CityDesk 2.0 release candidate, noting its stability after four rounds of beta testing and signaling a gold release within weeks.
key_points: There is an inherent tension between 'geeks' and 'suits' in every high-tech company
CityDesk 2.0 has reached release candidate status after four rounds of beta testing
Iterative beta testing with real users produces stable software releases
money_quotes: In every high tech company I've known, there's a war going on, between the geeks and the suits.
key_insight: The fundamental tension between technical and business perspectives is a universal dynamic in technology companies.
